title: BTS Tracker Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: Billboard officially confirmed ARIRANG debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard 200 with 641,000 equivalent album units (532K pure sales) — BTS's 7th chart-topping album and the biggest US album debut of 2026. 'SWIM' simultaneously topped the Billboard Hot 100 (BTS's 7th #1 single), making BTS the first Korean act to simultaneously lead both charts. 13 of ARIRANG's 14 tracks charted on the Hot 100 simultaneously, a K-pop record, while BTS occupied positions #1–#13 on the Billboard Global 200. 'Run BTS 2.0' was announced on March 30, with the variety show revival premiering April 7 on YouTube and Weverse ahead of the Arirang World Tour launch on April 9.

title: BTS Tracker Update — Mar 27, 2026
summary: BTS's landmark week: 'ARIRANG' (5th studio album, first since 2020) released March 20 to all-time records — 3.98M Hanteo Day 1 sales, 110M Spotify Day 1 streams (K-pop record), and all 14 tracks simultaneously topping Spotify's Global Top 50. The March 21 Gwanghwamun comeback concert drew ~260,000 in-person attendees and 18.4 million Netflix livestream viewers, becoming the largest public concert in South Korean history. BTS appeared on The Tonight Show two consecutive nights (March 25–26), including a surprise performance at the Guggenheim Museum. Netflix also released 'BTS: The Return' documentary on March 27. ARIRANG is tracking at 600K–625K US first-week units, on pace for the biggest US album debut of 2026.
